explanation_used:
1. **Opening statement**: This neuron strongly responds to **text that cites, quotes, or paraphrases official statements, reports, or communications from institutional authorities** (such as government/military officials, institutional spokespersons, official bulletins, or named reports from agencies).

2. **Domain coverage**: The pattern appears across news and reportage involving **governmental, military, legal, and public institutional contexts**. It is multilingual, with examples in English, Russian, Spanish, Italian, Dutch, Romanian, German, Arabic, Swedish, and Slovak. Topics include legal proceedings, military actions, diplomatic statements, official weather alerts, published investigations, and institutional announcements.

3. **Contrast with low-activation examples**: The high-activation texts consistently feature an **external, authoritative source framing the information** (e.g., "quoted one unnamed official," "said Bill DiSessa, a spokesman," "according to an internal investigation," "ha emesso un bollettino"). The low-activation examples describe events directly or discuss general topics (personal incidents, scientific discoveries, cultural topics) without this layer of formal citation or institutional voice.

4. **Closing interpretation**: The neuron detects a formal reporting convention where information is attributed to an organizational or authoritative entity. It focuses less on the specific subject matter and more on the **presence of an institutional voice or cited official source** within the text fragment.

results.actual_text: non hanno ovviamente la stessa capienza del Meazza, sono strutturati in maniera totalmente differente. Basti pensare all’
results.plain_text: Nel corso di un suo intervento negli studi di Sky Sport, Marco Bellinazzo ha parlato del nuovo stadio di Inter e Milan. Il Giuseppe Meazza, l’attuale impianto che ospita le due milanesi, è senza dubbio uno degli stadi più affascinanti del mondo. Gli oltre 80.000 posti a sedere, fanno di esso un pezzo più unico che raro.  Dunque, la domanda che sorge spontanea è: per quale motivo Inter e Milan dovrebbero abbandonare una struttura simile? L’intenzione delle due società, è di avviare uno stadio con gli standard moderni, concepito in “stile inglese”. Gli stadi britannici, che non hanno ovviamente la stessa capienza del Meazza, sono strutturati in maniera totalmente differente. Basti pensare all’Emirates Stadium dell’Arsenal (circa 60.000 posti), allo Stamford Bridge del Chelsea (circa 41.000 posti) o all’Etihad Stadium del Manchester City (circa 55.000 posti). Tutti impianti assolutamente affascinanti ma che, dal punto di vista della capienza, non hanno nulla a che vedere con lo Stadio San Siro.  Bellinazzo: “Col nuovo stadio Inter e Milan possono raggiungere 100 milioni di fatturato”  Sulla delicata tematica, il famoso esperto in economia calcistica ha dichiarato: “Lo stadio di proprietà è fondamentale: prima la Juventus incassava 12 milioni all’anno, ora dai 40 ai 60 milioni. Inter e Milan, che stanno discutendo del futuro San Siro, possono oggi mettere le basi per creare uno stadio che possa dare 100 milioni di fatturato all’anno, che potrebbe proiettarle nel calcio del futuro a partire dal 2024, quando si avrà la super Champions“.  Inter Dipendenza è fonte ufficiale di Google News. Rimani aggiornato, seguici qui

results.actual_text: ako nováčik súčasťou NHL. Toto v skutočnosti boli hráči, ktorí povedali
results.plain_text: Krsty nováčikov v NHL sú často šialené, spomína aj Gretzky  dnes | 15:40 BRATISLAVA - Hokejové tímy zámorskej NHL držia pokope nielen na ľade, ale aj mimo neho. Jedným z dôkazov je aj tradícia krstu nováčikov.   Nováčikovská večera je v najlepšej lige sveta tímovým vstupným ceremoniálom už takmer 40 rokov. Táto tradícia má svoj pôvod spätý s Edmontonom Oilers. Na začiatky tradície si zaspomínal aj Wayne Gretzky. Podrobnosti však prišili z Fínska, a to od bývalých, ale aj súčasných hviezd NHL.  Všetko sa to vraj začalo v sezóne 1979/1980. Aspoň tak to tvrdí ten najväčší hokejista všetkých čias, Wayne Gretzky.  „Boli sme výnimočný tím. Mali sme medzi sebou chlapíkov ako Colin Campbell, Lee Fogolin a Doug Hicks. Boli to úžasní páni. Dokázali k vám prísť a povedať vám, že už samo o sebe je to pre mladého chalana ťažké hrať v NHL, že nepotrebujú nikoho ponižovať, či oholiť mu hlavu za to, že je ako nováčik súčasťou NHL. Toto v skutočnosti boli hráči, ktorí povedali, poďme na večeru, všetci sa spoločne najeme, zaplatia to nováčikovia. A ako prichádzali ďalší noví hráči, pokračovali sme v tejto tradícii. Mysleli sme na to, že ich chceme prijať medzi seba a nie ponižovať, keďže sú súčasťou tímu tak ako ktorýkoľvek iný hráč,“ nechal sa počuť pre portál Sportsnet legendárny The Great One.     Z večere, kde skúsení hráči zastupovali rodičov, bola za čias legendárneho Fína Teemu Selänneho, vylúčená prítomnosť trénerov. A to najmä kvôli výške účtu. Nováčikovská večera prebiehala zvyčajne na tripe, teda v cudzom meste, kde sa tím zdržiaval viac dní. A práve na tieto chvíle si zaspomínala fínska hokejová ikona.  „Pamätám si na zopár účtov za približne 50-tisíc amerických dolárov. Starší hráči zvyčajne začínali pri výbere luxusnejších ročníkov vín. Raz som bol tuším účastníkom jednej takej večere, kde sa objavila fľaša koňaku za dvaapoltisíc. Keď je známy plán zápasov, všetkého sa ujme vodca kurzu. Podľa mňa je to čertovsky skvelá vec. Latka je nastavená vysoko a večera sa koná vo vychytených mestách ako je New York, Chicago alebo Vancouver,“ zaspomínal si s úsmevom Sellanne pre portále Yle.fi.   Fínsky blesk si odkrútil svoj debut v NHL v sezóne 1992/1993 v drese Winnipegu a v rámci nováčikovskej tradície pozval svojich spoluhráčov na kvalitné stejky. Účet bol 700 USD.  „Hráči v tom období nezarábali veľa. Ako si tak pamätám, musel som spievať a povedať vtip. Podstatou toho je, že nováčikovia si vedia vypiť,“ pokračoval Selänne.  Záver kariéry Teemu Selänneho bol v Anaheime spestrený príchodom nováčika Samiho Vatanena. Tím Anaheimu absolvoval v New Yorku nováčikovskú večeru, ktorá vstúpila do dejín.  Vatanen mal vo vianočnom kostýme vyjsť na Empire State Building alebo si urobiť ranný tréning na Times Square. „Áno. Bol som perníkový muž. Musel som si urobiť rozcvičku na Times Square. Kostým mi bol trochu tesný. Bola to však zábava. Na výšku účtu si nepamätám, ale bolo to dosť,“ nechal sa počuť Vatanen. Na záver môžeme už len dodať, že jeden náročný jesenný večer počas tohto roka zažil tím Columbusu Blue Jackets. V Los Angeles krstil hneď ôsmich nováčikov.   Zdroj: ŠPORT.SK, Jakub Slovák Foto: Getty Images

results.actual_text: erna izquierda. El ala cerrada de los Chicago Bears sufrió dislocación de rodilla y esto causó daños en una arteria,
results.plain_text: La jugada más escalofriante del fin de semana en la NFL fue la lesión de Zach Miller, cuya carrera corre peligro luego de ser operado de emergencia para buscar salvarle la pierna izquierda.  El ala cerrada de los Chicago Bears sufrió dislocación de rodilla y esto causó daños en una arteria, por lo que entró al quirófano para buscar evitar la amputación de la extremidad, según indicaron médicos del hospital donde fue intervenido.  Corría el tercer cuarto del partido en el que los Bears fueron derrotados en New Orleans por los Saints dentro de la semana ocho. La aparatosa lesión ocurrió en la zona de anotación con el esfuerzo de Zach Miller intentando convertir un pase del novato Mitchell Trubisky.

results.actual_text: When the depressing news broke last weekend that the latest Denon and Marantz AV amps
results.plain_text: When the depressing news broke last weekend that the latest Denon and Marantz AV amps and receivers had been hit by a bug that prevented them passing 4K/120Hz graphics from Xbox Series X consoles and Nvidia RTX 30 graphics cards through their HDMI 2.1 ports, it seemed likely that Yamaha’s next generation of AVRs was going to be impacted too. Now, alas, that has been confirmed.  Yamaha has today replied to a question I sent them about the issue last week with the following (slightly but I guess understandably vague) statement:  “We've been made aware of an issue related to certain emerging video signals (4K/120Hz etc) from certain sources that can cause a loss of picture and sound when passed through the AVR.  However, from the time we first announced our 2020 AVRs, we have stated that we will support such new formats via future updates.  Please rest assured that we will address the reported issue as part of these future updates including a permanent solution as needed.”  Yamaha’s statement also confirms that the affected models are the RX-V6A, the RX-V4A, the TSR-700 and the TSR-400, and that no date for the availability of the ‘future updates’ is currently available.  I asked Yamaha in response to its statement if it was suggesting that it believed the issue (which is not confirmed to also affect PS5s) might be resolvable via a firmware update, contrary to previous expectations. The reply to this follow-up question, however, was that Yamaha can’t confirm the method of the permanent solution at this time.  With this in mind, Yamaha has promised to update me with more information as its next steps become clearer. I’m also waiting on more information from Denon and Marantz owner Sound United on its plans for resolving the problems - so keep an eye on my Forbes channel for further news.  —  Related Reading

However, from the time we first announced our 2020 AVRs, we have stated that we will support such new formats via future updates. Please rest assured that we will address the reported issue as part of these future updates including a permanent solution as needed.”

results.actual_text: Shine Nurture Center, a childcare center in Mt. Airy, became worker
results.plain_text: Shine Nurture Center, a childcare center in Mt. Airy, became worker-owned this month, joining a growing wave of businesses helping to improve quality of life for workers and communities.  “For the workers, this transition has opened the door to building wealth for their families in a way that would never have been possible,” said Ellen Vera, director of co-op organizing at Co-op Cincy, a local nonprofit that supported the transition. “And for the community, this succession has anchored an important childcare center in the neighborhood long term.”  Katie McGoron founded Shine in 2015 with the goal of creating a childcare center where children could play outside and eat healthy food. Now that the business has a solid foundation, with a waiting list that typically includes 75 to 100 children, she decided to pursue a graduate degree. This month she finished the process of selling the business to 5 of her former employees.  McGoron wanted to give the employees an opportunity to make the business their own. “It’s just such a great group,” she said of the current team. “I really wanted to take myself out of it, let them take Shine in the direction they see fit. I honestly think it will get better and better.”  “The responsibility of a business like that on one person’s shoulders is a lot. When you can spread it over 5 shoulders, there’s more opportunity for growth.”  Shine’s 5 worker-owners, all of whom are women, will now run the childcare center by themselves. This business model, known as worker-ownership, has been shown to create resilient jobs, generate wealth, and boost the local economy.  “I’ve definitely been celebrating all month and telling my kids, ‘Your Momma owns a business!’” said worker-owner Mary Wilder, who has been at Shine for 2 years. Before now, paperwork discouraged her from owning a childcare center. She changed her mind because Shine was “such a wonderful place to work.”  “If I plan on being here for 10 years, why not be an owner?” she said.  Other worker-owners at Shine cited the benefits of the worker-ownership model and the strength of the childcare center’s team.  Beth Heeg, who has been with Shine for 1 year, was attracted to the worker-ownership model because of the emphasis on “wealth redistribution,” while Trisha Hay, who has worked at Shine for 3 years, said she decided to become a worker-owner because she felt she was never going to find another place like Shine. “I was all-in immediately,” she said. “I feel like we all communicate and work really well together.”  Why embrace the worker-owned business model? Worker-owned businesses are democratically run, giving workers a say. In addition, according to research, they have clear worker, company, and community benefits compared to traditional companies:  Employee advantage: Employees at worker-owned businesses have better wages and benefits. Company advantage: Worker-owned companies are more profitable and productive. Community advantage: Worker-owned businesses are less likely to close, relocate, or lay off workers during downturns. “We are really excited that Shine has decided to transition to a worker-owned co-op,” Vera said. “Selling to the workers was such a good option for the owner because she wanted to preserve her legacy and keep the business in the hands of the people who had helped her build it.”  “It also allowed the owner to get a fair market price for the value she had built up in her business over the years, which is especially important in an industry such as childcare that is not known for having a lot of outside buyers that are typically interested in taking over.”  To create a fairer and more resilient economy, Co-op Cincy’s Business Legacy Fund helps exiting or retiring business owners sell their companies to workers.  The fund, which supported Shine’s transition, helps save jobs and maintain legacies. Financing is accompanied by technical assistance. Co-op Cincy and the Business Legacy Fund partner with the Cincinnati USA Regional Chamber’s Minority Business Accelerator, the African-American Chamber of Greater Cincinnati-Northern Kentucky, the Greater Cincinnati Microenterprise Initiative, All-In-Cincinnati, and Xavier Leadership Center. The Business Legacy Fund is funded through the support of the Carol Ann and Ralph V. Haile, Jr. Foundation, the Greater Cincinnati Foundation, the Fifth Third Foundation and PNC Bank.  McGoron said Co-op Cincy offered an essential “support system” for Shine’s transition. “We could not have figured out how to do this without the help of Co-op Cincy,” McGoron said.  Co-op Cincy has recruited transition managers in Cincinnati and is actively conducting outreach to local businesses. In addition, Co-op Cincy partners with organizations across the state and country to promote worker-ownership.  coopcincy.org
